items.title: MBPP culls over 4,000 pest birds in May control operations
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Island City Council (MBPP) culled 4,424 pest birds and destroyed 125 crow nests last month as part of efforts to safeguard public health and urban cleanliness. MBPP said it remained committed to maintaining a clean, safe and healthy urban environment through continuous pest bird control operations across its jurisdiction. MBPP said it had also stepped up enforcement against those feeding wild birds, a key factor contributing to rising pest bird populations.

items.title: Cops plead not guilty to extorting RM10,000 from salesman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Four police corporals pleaded not guilty in the magistrate's court today to a charge of jointly extorting RM10,000 from a salesman by allegedly threatening him with a heavier court fine. Mohd Al Hafis Sairudin, 43, Mohd Azmi Mohd Ghozali, 40, Muhamad Azlan Zakaria, 39, and Muhammad Hanif Abdul Talib, 30, entered their pleas before magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan. The four allegedly intimidated the 35-year-old salesman by making him believe he faced a minimum court fine of RM50,000, inducing him to hand over RM10,000.

items.title: India beats growth forecasts but Iran war clouds outlook
items.summary: Gross domestic product expanded 7.8 per cent in the January-March quarter from a year earlier, according to official data released on Friday, exceeding market expectations of 7.3 per cent growth. Growth for the 2025-26 fiscal year came in at 7.7 per cent, up from 7.1 per cent the previous year, reaffirming India's position as the world's fastest-growing major economy. The country's central bank on Friday cut its 2026-27 growth forecast to 6.6 per cent from 6.9 per cent, while policymakers unveiled measures to attract foreign investment and support the rupee after overseas investors sold more than US$20 billion worth of Indian shares this year.

items.title: No discussions on reviving Muafakat Nasional 2.0, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today dismissed suggestions of reviving Muafakat Nasional (MN) 2.0, saying there have been no discussions on the matter between Umno and PAS. Ahmad Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, said Umno and Barisan Nasional’s (BN) priority remains ensuring political stability through the unity government formed with Pakatan Harapan (PH) and parties from Sabah and Sarawak. Yesterday, PAS Youth proposed that Umno revive political cooperation through Muafakat Nasional ahead of the upcoming Johor state election.

items.title: No nation on its own is able to quickly replace lost Middle East oil supply – Rosneft CEO
items.summary: ST PETERSBURG: No country, including the US, could quickly replace oil supplies lost as a result of the Middle East crisis, Rosneft chief executive Igor Sechin said on Friday. Asked whether oil prices could rise to US$150 per barrel if the Strait of Hormuz were closed, Sechin replied: "Ask comrade Trump." © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Pahang Sultan graces launch of gym for firefighters at Indera Mahkota Station
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 5 — The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah today officiated the opening of the Mahkota Gymnasium at the Indera Mahkota Fire and Rescue Station, a reflection of His Royal Highness’ concern for the fitness and well-being of rescue personnel. The Pahang Ruler arrived at about 3pm and was welcomed by Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department director-general Nor Hisham Mohammad, Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim and Universal Fitness & Leisure Sdn Bhd (UFL) managing director Datuk Radha Krishnan. The Sultan later received a briefing from Mohd Razam on the development and operation of the gymnasium, which is equipped with exercise equipment donated by His Royal Highness.

items.title: Govt targets 650,000 TVET students by year-end, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government is targeting 650,000 technical and vocational education and training (TVET) students across 1,395 institutions nationwide by Dec 31, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. Zahid, who is also National TVET Council chairman, said about 587,000 students enrolled in TVET institutions last year, compared with about 121,000 when the council was first established. On National TVET Day, Zahid said the initiative aimed to showcase TVET institutions nationwide and promote high-quality programmes to students and prospective applicants.

items.title: Oil edges lower after Oman says Mina al Fahal operations proceeding normally
items.summary: LONDON: Oil prices edged lower after Oman said operations at Mina al Fahal port were proceeding normally, following a Reuters report that oil loadings had been suspended after an explosion. Brent crude futures fell by 50 cents, or 0.53 per cent, to US$94.53 a barrel by 0915 GMT after settling down 2.84 per cent in the previous session. US West Texas Intermediate crude was at US$92.61 a barrel, down 43 cents, or 0.46 per cent, following a 3.1 per cent loss on Thursday.

items.title: Transparency International Malaysia warns against overuse of corruption settlements, 'pay-to-settle' culture
items.summary: Its president, Dr Raymon Ram, said the increasing reliance on such mechanisms without clear public justification could erode trust in anti-corruption institutions and raise questions over accountability. Raymon said TI-M recognised that Malaysian law provides for compounds and other discretionary mechanisms, and acknowledged the importance of recovering public funds and proceeds of unlawful activities. Raymon said TI-M had consistently cautioned that such outcomes could undermine public confidence in accountability and the equal application of the law.

items.title: Middle East conflict pushing millions into hunger, WFP says
items.summary: GENEVA: The Middle East conflict is pushing millions of people closer to hunger, as rising fuel and transport costs drive up food prices while funding shortfalls force aid agencies to scale back assistance, the UN World Food Programme said on Friday. In March, the WFP forecast as many as 45 million people could fall into acute food insecurity if oil prices remained around US$100 per barrel through June. In Somalia, 6.5 million people, roughly a third of the population, are expected to face severe hunger in 2026, while Afghanistan could see 17.4 million people affected, the WFP said.

items.title: BN open to continuing unity government with Pakatan in Negeri Sembilan, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is ready for the Negeri Sembilan state election and remains open to continuing its cooperation with Pakatan Harapan (PH) to retain the unity government in the state, its chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. In the 2023 state election, PH and BN formed the Negeri Sembilan government after securing a combined 31 of the 36 seats contested. Negeri Sembilan became the second state to dissolve its legislative assembly for a fresh election after Johor, which dissolved its State Legislative Assembly on Monday.

items.title: Ringgit weighed down by rising fuel prices, geopolitical risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar on Friday amid cautious sentiment over lingering geopolitical uncertainty in West Asia and rising fuel prices. He said authorities across Asia have also reaffirmed their commitment to stabilising their respective currencies amid heightened market volatility. The local currency traded lower against regional peers.

items.title: Pahang Sultan urges on-site gyms for uniformed personnel
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang has called on uniformed agencies to incorporate gyms within their facilities to help personnel maintain physical fitness and perform their duties effectively. Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah said personnel from the police, armed forces, navy, air force and Fire and Rescue Department should make full use of such facilities to stay in optimal health. "Uniformed government agencies, especially frontline services, should provide on-site facilities that enable personnel to improve their well-being and fitness.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 5, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today as bargain hunting continued after the recent strong sell-off. Buying support may continue to support the local market, but investors are likely to stay selective due to geopolitical risks and global economic uncertainty. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Zeekr partners DC Handal to expand EV charging network in Malaysia
items.summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Malaysia has signed a pact with Handal Green Mobility Sdn Bhd (DC Handal) to collaborate on expanding access to high-speed electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure nationwide. It is aimed at improving charging accessibility for more than 4,000 Zeekr vehicle owners here as the company seeks to strengthen its after-sales ecosystem beyond vehicle sales. Zeekr said it will leverage DC Handal's existing network of direct current (DC) fast-charging stations located across key transport routes.

items.title: PN too divided for expansion, says Puad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas' proposed Perikatan Nasional Plus (PN+) formula is unrealistic political rhetoric that is unlikely to materialise amid ongoing tensions within the coalition, said Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi. He described the idea of an expanded coalition as "mere rhetoric", citing existing fractures within Perikatan Nasional (PN), including the emergence of the Internal Political Reform (IPR) movement. "PN+ is nothing more than unrealistic political rhetoric.

items.title: DayOne closes US$4.5bil Series C as data centre expansion accelerates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Singapore-based digital infrastructure platform DayOne Data Centers has completed its Series C equity fundraising, securing US$4.5 billion in gross proceeds. New participants include the Indonesia Investment Authority and Achi Capital Partners. The company said the latest funding will support expansion across key markets including Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Japan, Hong Kong, Finland and Spain.

items.title: Eita Resources bags RM40.93mil contract for Johor data centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eita Resources Bhd has secured a RM40.93 million contract via its subsidiary Eita Power System Sdn Bhd for the supply and delivery of busduct systems for a data centre project in Johor. The contract runs from May to September this year. Eita said the contract is expected to contribute to its earnings for the financial year ending Sept 30, 2026.

items.title: PH-BN solo contests in Johor, Negri seen as key test ahead of future polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan's (PH) and Barisan Nasional's (BN) decision to contest separately in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections is expected to test each coalition's grassroots strength and voter support, say political analysts. Universiti Malaya political analyst Dr Mohammad Tawfik Yaakub said the prospect of PH and BN contesting separately in future state elections, and even the 16th General Election (GE16), was becoming increasingly realistic. "PH wants to test its own strength, grassroots machinery and voter support without relying on BN," he said.

items.title: BYD updates Atto 3 as EV adoption gains traction in Malaysia
items.summary: BYD Sime Motors has introduced the updated 2026 BYD Atto 3 at the Sime Motors MotorExpo 2026, as the model enters a refreshed phase in Malaysia's expanding electric vehicle (EV) market. The launch comes as BYD strengthens its position in the local EV segment, having surpassed 32,000 cumulative vehicle sales across its model range, according to BYD Sime Motors. BYD's current line-up in Malaysia includes models such as the Dolphin, Atto 3, Seal, M6, Sealion 7, Atto 2 and Seal 6, covering entry-level to larger family-oriented segments.

items.title: Nearly 600 smoking notices issued in George Town Heritage Zone since 2022, says Penang rep
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 5 — The Penang State Health Department (JKNPP) has issued 597 notices for smoking within the George Town UNESCO World Heritage Site here, through 114 enforcement operations since 2022. State Youth, Sports and Health Committee chairman Daniel Gooi Zi Sen said the world heritage site was gazetted as a smoke-free zone since 2015 and is now subjected to the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852). “Throughout last year, 106 notices were issued.

items.title: Myra Embun targets suburban demand shift in Putrajaya fringe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Oriental Interest Bhd, through its residential property arm Myra, has launched Myra Embun mixed development in Jenderam, Selangor as demand in the housing market shifts towards more connected suburban locations. Set on 2.06 hectares of freehold land, the project comprises serviced residences, retail components and a boutique hotel. The launch comes amid a more selective residential market, with National Property Information Centre data showing buyers increasingly prioritising connectivity, functionality and integrated developments, particularly in stratified housing segments.

items.title: UMS Integration eyes Vietnam precision engineering push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UMS Integration Ltd has signed a memorandum of understanding with two founding shareholders in Vietnam to explore a proposed restructuring and investment in precision engineering businesses. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the deal involves Viet Nguyen Mechanical Precision Co Ltd, Central Metal Mechanical Co Ltd and Global Metal Plating Co Ltd, which are expected to be consolidated into a newly-formed entity. The targeted businesses are involved in metal processing, plating and precision engineering, primarily serving the semiconductor and related industries in Vietnam.

items.title: Fahmi: MyKhas access suspension not arbitrary, rakyat not affected
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The suspension of access to the MyKhas system involving two members of Parliament was not arbitrary, and likely due to specific circumstances, says Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The unity government spokesman said the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) under the Prime Minister's Department had clarified that residents in the affected constituencies could still submit applications as usual, with approvals processed directly by the agency. "The issue relates to MyKhas allocations.

items.title: Nigerian student discharged over cannabis possession charge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Nigerian student was acquitted and discharged by the Sessions Court in Petaling Jaya today of a charge of possessing 54.39 grammes of cannabis after the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case against him. Judge Dr Syahliza Warnoh ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove a prima facie case at the close of its case and ordered that the 35-year-old accused be acquitted and discharged without being called to enter his defence. The court subsequently ordered that the accused be acquitted and discharged.

items.title: Guan Eng: Karpal Singh reclamation project was direct award, not open tender
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Lim Guan Eng claims that the 70-acre Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project was directly awarded and not subjected to an open tender process, escalating a tit-for-tat with Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. The former Penang chief minister said Chow's remarks effectively supported his earlier claim that the reclamation was not included in the Joint Development Agreement (JDA) signed on Feb 21, 2020. The Penang government had earlier denied Lim's claim that the project was awarded without an open tender.

items.title: EC to hold special meeting on June 12 to discuss Johor state election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — A special meeting to discuss matters related to the 16th Johor state election will be held on June 12, according to the Election Commission (EC). EC secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said the commission had received notification from Johor State Assembly Speaker Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi regarding the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 2, as provided under Clause 23(2), Part Two of the Johor Constitution 1895. “Under Clause 23(4), Part Two of the Johor Constitution 1895, a state election must be held within 60 days from the date of the dissolution of the State Assembly.

items.title: Two bomb threats in 48 hours: Taylor's campus declared safe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Taylor's University Lakeside Campus was declared safe this morning after a bomb threat prompted an evacuation and a comprehensive security sweep by authorities. The university said it activated its emergency response procedures immediately upon receiving the threat, with campus access restricted while security teams and police conducted checks across the premises. View on Threads The incident came a day after a bomb threat at the Sunway campus of Monash University Malaysia prompted an evacuation and security sweep by police.

items.title: EC receives notice on dissolution of Negri Sembilan state assembly
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Election Commission (EC) has received official notification of the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan state assembly. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the notice, dated today, triggered the process for a state election, which must be held within 60 days. The withdrawal saw Aminuddin lose majority support in the 36-seat state assembly.

items.title: Johor election polling date to be known next week
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Election Commission (EC) will convene a special meeting next Friday to discuss matters related to the upcoming 16th Johor election. EC secretary, Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus, said the meeting would be chaired by EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun to decide key dates for the polls, such as the nomination and polling dates. Khairul Shahril said a press conference would be called as soon as the meeting concludes.

items.title: Gold heads for weekly loss on fading Middle East peace hopes
items.summary: Spot gold was down 0.2 per cent at US$4,463.73 per ounce at 0849 GMT. The metal has fallen about 1.6 per cent so far this week. US gold futures for August delivery fell 0.3 per cent to US$4,491.

items.title: [UPDATED] BN ready for Negri Sembilan polls, open to unity govt talks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Barisan Nasional says it is prepared to contest the Negri Sembilan election following the dissolution of the state assembly under the Pakatan Harapan-led administration. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the coalition remained open to continuing cooperation within the unity government in the state. "We are ready to face the election and are open to maintaining the unity government in Negri Sembilan," he said.

items.title: Yen pinned near intervention zone, dollar boosted by Gulf tensions
items.summary: LONDON: The Japanese yen tested the 160-per-dollar barrier on Friday, drawing sharp official warnings, as the dollar held firm ahead of key US employment data and Middle East tensions underpinned safe-haven demand. Peace talks between the US and Iran are at a stalemate, and a reignition of hostilities this week has kept oil above US$90 a barrel, raising risks to global growth. The yen was last at 159.93 per dollar.

items.title: [UPDATED] Zahid: Umno–Pas talks routine, no involvement of party presidents
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The meeting between Umno and Pas leaders last night did not involve the presidents of either party, says Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the engagement was part of routine political discussions on potential areas of cooperation aimed at preserving political stability. Pas, Umno leaders held meeting last night, said Hadi Harakahdaily reported that Hadi, however, said he was not involved, describing it as a routine political meeting, adding: "We meet anyone who wants to meet us."

items.title: Four KL cops plead not guilty to extorting salesman of RM10,000
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Four police corporals pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of extorting RM10,000 from a salesman three years ago. Mohd Al Hafis Sairudin, 43, Mohd Azmi Mohd Ghozali, 40, Muhamad Azlan Zakaria, 39, and Muhammad Hanif Abdul Talib, 30, were jointly charged with extorting Danny Goh Ngee Guan, 35, by allegedly causing him to fear that he would be fined at least RM50,000 by the court. The court fixed July 8 for mention.

items.title: Blockbuster SpaceX IPO set to test high-flying US stocks rally
items.summary: NEW YORK: The long-awaited, massive SpaceX initial public offering is expected next week, a major event for the US stock market, with investors wary of possible overexuberance after a stunning rally. Investors will also assess fresh inflation data and earnings reports from key companies in the technology sector, which has driven the market's recent surge. "Nothing has stuck in terms of pessimism in the last two months," said Mark Hackett, chief market strategist for Nationwide.

items.title: Monash, Taylor’s say campuses back to ‘normal’ after bomb scares, confirm cops gave safety clearance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Both Taylor’s University and Monash University Malaysia said this afternoon that their campuses have resumed normal operations, after police confirmed that it was safe to do so. Earlier today, Taylor’s University campus in Subang Jaya was reportedly evacuated. In its latest update this afternoon, Monash University Malaysia said it had “resumed normal campus operations” today, following a thorough security assessment by local authorities in response to yesterday’s reported bomb threat.

items.title: EC receives notice of Negeri Sembilan assembly dissolution, says special meeting to set poll dates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The Election Commission (EC) has received official notification of the dissolution of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly today. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the commission received a letter from Negeri Sembilan State Assembly Speaker Datuk M K Ibrahim Abd Rahman notifying it of the dissolution of the 15th State Legislative Assembly under Article 56(2) of the state constitution. He said Article 56(4) of the state constitution requires a state election to be held within 60 days of the dissolution of the assembly.

items.title: MyKhas suspension does not affect MPs’ rights, says Fahmi
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Government spokesperson Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has stressed that the suspension of access to the MyKhas portal for the Members of Parliament (MPs) for Petaling Jaya and Subang does not affect their rights or functions as elected representatives. The communications minister also said residents in both constituencies would continue to have access to assistance despite the restrictions. “The Implementation Coordination Unit of the Prime Minister’s Department (ICU JPM) has clarified that residents in both parliamentary constituencies may still submit applications, which will be assessed and approved directly by the ICU.

items.title: PM Anwar directs all government digital services to be developed in-house, says Fahmi
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed that all aspects of government digital service development be carried out internally to safeguard data security and sovereignty. Madani Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, who is also communications minister, said the prime minister conveyed the directive during the Cabinet meeting today. Fahmi said the internal development of digital services would be coordinated by the Ministry of Digital through the National Digital Department.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz performs Friday prayers at Masjid Kariah Gunung Pasir in Seri Menanti
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 5 — The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today performed Friday prayers at Masjid Kariah Gunung Pasir in Seri Menanti. His Royal Highness was accompanied by his sons, Tunku Besar of Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin and Tunku Zain Al-’Abidin. The prayers were led by Masjid Kariah Gunung Pasir's Imam 1, Wan Muhammad Saifuddin Wan Salim.

items.title: Police arrest 18 men after two Kampung Air fights, one stabbed with stingray spine stable
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 5 — Police have arrested 18 men to assist in investigations into two fights that occurred in Kampung Air early Thursday morning. Acting Kota Kinabalu district police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Abdul Rahman said initial investigations found that the incident stemmed from a disagreement between several individuals before escalating into a fight. Syed Lot said police subsequently arrested 18 men believed to have been involved in the incidents.

items.title: Kuching man jailed one month, fined RM1,500 for stealing Wagyu beef and salmon trout at supermarket
items.summary: KUCHING, June 5 — A 28-year-old man who stole two Australian Wagyu beef cuts and two salmon trout from a supermarket here was sentenced to one month’s imprisonment and fined RM1,500, in default one month’s jail. Based on the facts of the case, a complainant at the shopping centre noticed a suspicious-looking man, later identified as the accused, leaving the premises. The complainant, together with a security guard, subsequently apprehended the man on suspicion of stealing items from the supermarket.

items.title: Perlis Malaysia Sports School abandoned 12 years now 60pc complete, slated for January 2027 opening
items.summary: KANGAR, June 5 — The long-delayed Perlis Malaysia Sports School project in Bukit Chabang near Padang Besar is expected to be completed by January 2027, according to Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah. “I met the Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, last March, and he was very committed to helping Perlis and resolving the issue involving the Perlis Sports School. On April 27 last year, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, in a Facebook post, stated that the Perlis Malaysia Sports School project, which had been abandoned for the past 12 years, should be handed over immediately to a capable government-linked company to ensure its completion.

items.title: No-confidence vote against MB Abu Bakar fizzles out as Perlis legislative assembly ends
items.summary: KANGAR, June 5 — Rumours of a no-confidence motion against Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah never materialised as the Perlis state assembly wrapped up its three-day sitting today. Abu Bakar said this should end the polemics dogging his administration and prove that Perikatan Nasional (PN) representatives, especially from PAS and Bersatu, are still working together, Harian Metro reported this afternoon. The Bersatu politician also expressed his appreciation to all PN assemblymen, especially from PAS, for their full commitment in not bringing such a motion.

items.title: Authorities bar public from Gunung Batu Putih search, missing hiker Jaslinda hunt restricted to pros for safety
items.summary: IPOH, June 5 — Members of the public and non-Malim Gunung Perhutanan (MGP) members are barred from joining the search and rescue operation for a female hiker missing at Gunung Batu Putih, Tapah, on safety grounds. Malaysian Mountain Guides Association (PMGM) chairman, Muzafar Mohamad, said the decision was made because the Trans Beratih route, which is the focus of the search, is a relatively new trail unfamiliar to many, including some members of the MGP. Muzafar said the police, Fire and Rescue Department, Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM), the General Operations Force (GOF) and the state Forestry Department all supported restricting participation in the operation to minimise the risk of accidents.

items.title: Fahmi rules out national snap polls, says Cabinet remains stable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The federal government today dismissed speculation of an early nationwide poll despite the dissolution of the state legislative assemblies in Johor and Negeri Sembilan. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said there have been no discussions on holding an early general election. Fahmi said the federal coalition government remains stable and unaffected by recent developments.

items.title: Perlis MB cautiously welcomes Muafakat revival amid election buzz, buries SD row
items.summary: KANGAR, June 5 — Perlis Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Hamzah today said the state government will adopt a wait-and-see approach following a meeting between PAS and Umno leaders in Kuala Lumpur, stressing that any political development must place Malay unity at the forefront. He said Malay-based political parties should work together and remain united in defending the rights and interests of the community, Utusan Malaysia reported this afternoon. Abdul Hadi had also said efforts to revive Muafakat Nasional were consistent with PAS’ long-standing objective of strengthening Malay-Muslim political cooperation.

items.title: Penang council swoops in, fines 17 for feeding pigeons and crows, culls thousands
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 5 — The Penang Island City Council (MBPP) issued 17 compounds last month to individuals found feeding pigeons and crows as part of efforts to curb the growing population of pest birds in urban areas. In a statement today, MBPP said feeding birds was among the key factors contributing to the increasing population of pest birds, particularly pigeons and crows, on the island. “Throughout May, MBPP also conducted continuous control operations, during which 1,817 pigeons and 2,607 crows were culled, while 125 crow nests were destroyed to prevent a further increase in their population,” the statement said.

items.title: MAF confirms Malbatt personnel safe after Lebanon mortar attack kills Spanish UN peacekeeper
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) has confirmed that all personnel of the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 serving in southern Lebanon are safe and unaffected following a mortar attack on UNP 7-2, the Spanish Battalion (Spanbatt) position in the Eastern Sector, on Wednesday. In a statement today, the MAF said the incident, which occurred at 11.20pm local time, left three Spanbatt peacekeepers injured, while one of them was confirmed dead yesterday morning. “The MAF extends its condolences to the family of the fallen Spanbatt peacekeeper and prays for the swift recovery of those injured,” the statement said.

items.title: Noor Hisam moves to Bukit Aman, Mohd Alwi steps up as Perak police chief
items.summary: IPOH, June 5 — Penang deputy police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin has been appointed as the new Perak chief police officer, effective today. The handover of duties ceremony, held at the Senior Police Officers’ Mess in Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah here, was witnessed by Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri. Mohd Alwi replaces Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin, who has taken over as director of the Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department.

items.title: Perikatan’s ‘pluses and minuses’ not my concern, Anwar says amid PAS push to broaden ties
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today dismissed talk of Perikatan Nasional (PN) expanding into a broader political pact, saying he had no interest in tracking the Opposition’s political arithmetic. Anwar said his focus remained on running the government, not on speculation over PN’s attempts to court other Malay-based parties. Anwar was asked to comment on PN’s reported proposal to form “PN Plus” by bringing in other Malay-based parties.

items.title: High Court grants Najib partial access to 1MDB documents in US$8b suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The High Court here today partially allowed an application by former Prime Minister Datuk Seri Najib Tun Razak to obtain several documents ahead of the hearing of the US$8 billion civil suit filed by 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) and its subsidiaries against him and seven others. Judicial Commissioner Mohamad Redzuan Idrus granted Najib access to five categories of documents, including a complete list of all monies, assets and properties recovered, returned, repatriated or realised, as well as documents detailing the terms and conditions of settlements relating to 1MDB’s financial losses arising from alleged abuse of power and breaches of fiduciary duties. The suit, filed on May 7, 2021, initially named 15 individuals, including Najib, as defendants.

items.title: Bukit Aman investigating claim Malaysian woman set up by daughter in Nigeria cannabis arrest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Police are investigating claims that a 63-year-old woman detained by Nigerian authorities for allegedly attempting to smuggle 31 kilogrammes (kg) of Canadian Loud cannabis into the country had been set up by her daughter. Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the claim is under investigation and efforts to trace the woman’s daughter are ongoing. International media previously reported that the woman was detained by Nigerian authorities on May 17 after allegedly attempting to smuggle 31kg of Canadian Loud cannabis into the country through Murtala Muhammed International Airport in Lagos.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz is still Negeri Sembilan Ruler, Anwar says despite Melaka hotel proclamation
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed Putrajaya’s recognition of Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir as the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, as the state’s royal succession dispute widened into the courts, the police and now a snap election. “Based on the constitutional line of succession as it stands, we continue to recognise Tuanku Muhriz as the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan. Anwar said while individuals have the right to seek remedies under the law and state constitution, attempts to remove a ruler carry far-reaching implications for the country.

items.title: Muhyiddin not in Johor state poll plans, says state Perikatan chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin will not be making a return to contest in the upcoming Johor election, according to Johor Perikatan Nasional (PN) chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal. The confirmation comes amid mounting speculation over candidate line-ups after Johor's state assembly was dissolved this week ahead of fresh polls. He said there had been no discussions within Johor PN regarding the former Gambir assemblyman contesting any state seat.

items.title: Anwar backs Negeri Sembilan assembly dissolution, says Johor polls came sooner than hoped
items.summary: PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said he would have preferred Johor to delay its state election by a few months to allow the government to focus on economic recovery and addressing people’s concerns. The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1, paving the way for the state election to be held within 60 days. In the 2022 Johor state election, Barisan Nasional won 40 seats, PH secured 12, Perikatan Nasional won three, while Muda captured one.

items.title: Transparency International Malaysia warns against ‘pay-to-settle’ perception in graft cases, calls for clearer oversight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Transparency International Malaysia (TI-M) today expressed concern over the increasing use of compounds, settlements and charge withdrawals in corruption-related cases, warning that repeated reliance on such mechanisms without adequate public explanation risks weakening confidence in anti-corruption institutions and perceptions of accountability. While acknowledging that Malaysian law permits compounds and other discretionary mechanisms in certain circumstances, TI-M said there must be greater transparency, consistency and public confidence in how such powers are exercised. “While asset recovery serves an important public interest objective, recovery alone should not be regarded as a substitute for accountability,” TI-M president Raymon Ram said in a statement.

items.title: Malaysia denounces Israel’s violations in Lebanon, urges protection of UN peacekeepers and civilians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Malaysia has condemned the continuing attacks on United Nations peacekeepers in Lebanon and extended its condolences to Serbia following the death of a Serbian peacekeeper serving with the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil). In a statement on Friday, the Foreign Ministry said Malaysia stood in solidarity with Unifil and condemned in the strongest terms the continuing attacks targeting United Nations peacekeepers. Malaysia is among the countries contributing personnel to Unifil, which operates in southern Lebanon under a United Nations mandate.

items.title: Taxi driver claims trial to cannabis trafficking charge in KL High Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — A taxi driver pleaded not guilty in the High Court here today to a charge of trafficking 748.59 grammes of cannabis last year. Mohd Faris Ali, 55, entered the plea after the charge was read out to him before Judge Datuk Aslam Zainuddin. He is accused of trafficking the drug at Jalan 3/149J, Bandar Baru Seri Petaling, Kuala Lumpur, at about 5 pm on Jan 23, 2025.
